features.summary: UnitedHealth Group reported strong Q3 2022 results with revenues of $81 billion (12% growth) and raised full-year adjusted EPS guidance to $21.85-$22.05. OptumHealth revenue grew 34% driven by value-based care expansion, while UnitedHealthcare added 800,000 Medicare Advantage members year-to-date. The company completed the Change Healthcare acquisition, combining it with OptumInsight to enhance technology capabilities. Management highlighted new partnerships with Walmart for value-based care clinics and initiatives to improve healthcare affordability, including zero co-pay diabetes medications.
features.management_outlook: Management expressed confidence in 2023 performance, noting that current analyst consensus estimates are at the top end of their likely initial guidance range. They maintain their long-term 13% to 16% EPS growth target and see strong momentum across business segments. OptumHealth's value-based care platform and Medicare Advantage plans are positioned for continued strong growth. The company has deployed $20 billion in M&A capital in 2022 versus $5 billion in 2021, with substantial capacity for additional investments.
features.risks: Key risks include potential membership disruption from Medicaid redeterminations resuming in 2023, requiring significant outreach efforts to maintain coverage continuity. Medical cost trends are expected to run higher than historical norms due to inflation, labor costs, and care pattern normalization. The company faces ongoing uncertainty around utilization patterns and potential economic slowdown impacts on commercial membership. Integration costs and investments in Change Healthcare acquisition may pressure near-term margins.
features.key_drivers: Growth is driven by OptumHealth's expanding value-based care relationships serving 20 million lives with under 15% in full capitation arrangements, providing significant runway for revenue per consumer growth. Medicare Advantage membership growth of 800,000 year-to-date reflects strong market positioning with 81% of members in 4+ star plans. The Change Healthcare integration creates opportunities for enhanced technology solutions and administrative simplification. Commercial business shows resilience with net hiring among customers and strong national accounts renewals for 2023.
